basics.name: Dr Morgan John Brand
basics.label: Generalist Scientist · Microbiology & Systems
basics.summary: A generalist scientist and PhD graduate. I own and live on a stretch of the KwaZulu-Natal coast; I grow bananas on it my own way, and I'm less interested in farming the land than in coming to understand it — reading it closely enough to work with how it already behaves. Microbiology and systems are things I experience here first-hand, not only study. Trained through aquaculture and microbiology, I think in systems: soil, water, data, and the microbial life that binds them together. I move easily between the lab, the field, and the codebase, with work spanning reproducible data science, applied AI, geospatial analysis, and the living systems of the land itself. What connects it is a scientist's instinct for how a whole system behaves, and a builder's habit of turning messy real-world data into something clear enough to act on.

work.name: Canaan
work.summary: My own land on the KwaZulu-Natal coast — I live on it and grow bananas by my own methods.
highlights: Grow, harvest, pack, and move bananas
Data capture and analysis of the land and its systems

work.name: University of Cape Town
work.summary: Postgraduate research training (aquaculture, microbiology, data science).
highlights: Awarded NRF Research Scholarship for scarce skills
Part-time lecturing and teaching of undergraduate coursework
Part of the original social-media team for the @Ma-Re (MARE_UCT) account

education.thesis: Ulva as a functional feed: a practical investigation into the effects of Ulva on the growth, consumption, health, and microbiome of the farmed abalone Haliotis midae.
Evaluation of algae, grown in brewery effluent water, as a potential dietary ingredient for the farmed abalone Haliotis midae.

publications.name: Why grow Ulva? Its potential role in the future of aquaculture
publications.authors: Bolton JJ, Cyrus MD, Brand MJ, Joubert M, Macey B

communication.title: Morgs Brew (YouTube channel)
communication.summary: Created and ran a 38-video technical how-to channel translating hands-on science and engineering for a general audience: small-scale growing, water systems (DIY ram pumps, drip irrigation, rainwater harvesting), fermentation and home-brewing, and soil, microbiome and DIY tool-making.

communication.title: Zero to Hero: a change of perspective
communication.summary: Invited guest feature — a popular-science essay on the reproductive strategy of the sea catfish (barbel), written during Rhodes University honours.

communication.title: Fresh Rots — science & land blog
communication.summary: Self-built personal blog (authored in R with blogdown/Hugo) publishing genuine first-person posts under my own name and long-standing "Morgs Brew" handle — spanning marine biology, a TLUD-pyrolysis biochar technical guide, ram-pump irrigation, and rainfall and erosion records from the land.

communication.title: DIY SauerKraut Workshop: Growing your guts superpowers
communication.summary: Co-created and co-presented a public science workshop on fermentation and the gut microbiome, released under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International licence.
